revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:出雲祝神社]] {{Otheruses|A shrine located in Iruma City|Other uses|Izumo Iwai Shrine (disambiguation)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Izumo Iwai Shrine |image = File:Izumoiwai-jinja(Iruma,Miyadera).jpg |location = {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|qid=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}{{ill|Iruma, Saitama|en|Iruma, Saitama|qid=Q696925|lt=Iruma, Saitama|1=Iruma, Saitama}} Miyadera 1 |latitude_deg = 35 |latitude_min = 47 |latitude_sec = 16.0 |longitude_deg = 139 |longitude_min = 22 |longitude_sec = 35.7 |deities = {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi|ja|天穂日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hohi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ|1=Ame no Hohi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10940685}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Hinatori|ja|天夷鳥命|1=Takehi-Nateru|8=fr|10=zh|11=建比良鳥命|lt=Ame-no-Hinatori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hinatori|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 建比良鳥命}}
{{ill|E-tamo-hi-no-Mikoto|ja|兄多毛比命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/E-tamo-hi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |sacred_object = |shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}
{{ill|Village shrine (Japan)|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine (Japan)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} |established = Unknown |chief_priest = |main_building = Nagare-zukuri style |alias = Yorigimiya (Shrine of Yori Trees) |pilgrimage = |annual_festival = November 11 |rituals = |map = Japan Saitama |location = {{ill|Iruma|qid=Q696925|1=Iruma, Saitama}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.78777777777778|N|139.37658333333334|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Izumo Iwai Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|出雲祝神社}}, "Izumo Iwai Jinja") is a Shinto shrine located along the Furō River in {{ill|Iruma, Saitama|en|Iruma, Saitama|qid=Q696925|lt=Iruma, Saitama|1=Iruma, Saitama}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|qid=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}, Japan. The shrine enshrines three primary deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi|ja|天穂日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hohi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ|1=Ame no Hohi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10940685}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Hinatori|ja|天夷鳥命|1=Takehi-Nateru|8=fr|10=zh|11=建比良鳥命|lt=Ame-no-Hinatori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hinatori|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 建比良鳥命}}, and {{ill|E-tamo-hi-no-Mikoto|ja|兄多毛比命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/E-tamo-hi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It is one of the candidate shrines for the ancient Shikinaisha "Izumo Iwai Shrine" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, and it held the former shrine Rank of {{ill|Village shrine (Japan)|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine (Japan)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}. == History == The shrine was reportedly founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|qid=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}} (71–130 AD).According to the historical signboard at the first torii (Installed on January 20, 1953). According to legend, during his eastern expedition, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q461258}} stopped at {{ill|Kotesashi Plain|ja|小手指原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotesashi Plain|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小手指町|1=Kotesashi-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11460796}} and enshrined {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi|ja|天穂日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hohi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ|1=Ame no Hohi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10940685}} and {{ill|Ame-no-Hinatori|ja|天夷鳥命|1=Takehi-Nateru|8=fr|10=zh|11=建比良鳥命|lt=Ame-no-Hinatori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hinatori|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 建比良鳥命}}. It is also said that {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi|ja|天穂日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hohi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ|1=Ame no Hohi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10940685}}, who traveled from the eastern lands, sowed seeds from driftwood that had floated from {{ill|Kitsuki Bay|ja|杵築湾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsuki Bay|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 守江湾|1=Morie Bay|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11450088}}. The resulting forest became known as "Yorigi no Mori" (Forest of Yori Trees).Signboard at the first torii "Izumo Iwai Shrine Forest – Furusato no Mori" (Installed on March 31, 1994). A surviving roof beam plaque from the shrine's rebuilding in 702 AD (Daihō 2) bears an inscription that reads "Iwai" (伊波比). :'''Inscribed Plaque''' :''「伊都毛伊波比再造 牟射志入間臣宇助 大寶二年壬寅九月廿九日」'' ("Rebuilt as Izumo Iwai Shrine, Usa from Iruma, Musashi Province, Daihō 2, 29th day of the 9th month") The shrine's first torii, built in 1828 (Bunsei 11), has a plaque inscribed with "Yorigi-no-Miya" (Shrine of Yori Trees), giving rise to the name of the surrounding area, Miyadera Village (宮寺村). In 1869 (Meiji 2), the shrine was officially renamed '''Izumo Iwai Shrine'''. === Shrine Status (Shakaku) === * 1872 (Meiji 5) - Ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine (Japan)|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine (Japan)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} File:Izumoiwai-jinja(Iruma,Miyadera) Torii1.jpg|First torii File:Izumoiwai-jinja(Iruma,Miyadera) Sando.jpg|Pathway (Sando) File:Izumoiwai-jinja(Iruma,Miyadera) Torii2.jpg|Second torii File:Izumoiwai-jinja(Iruma,Miyadera) Kaguraden.jpg|Kaguraden (Dance Hall) == Enshrined Deities == '''Main Deities (Shusaijin)''' * {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi|ja|天穂日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hohi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ|1=Ame no Hohi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10940685}} (Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto)Historical Board at the Worship Hall (Installed on September 29, 1951) * {{ill|Ame-no-Hinatori|ja|天夷鳥命|1=Takehi-Nateru|8=fr|10=zh|11=建比良鳥命|lt=Ame-no-Hinatori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hinatori|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 建比良鳥命}} (Ame-no-Hinatori-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|E-tamo-hi-no-Mikoto|ja|兄多毛比命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/E-tamo-hi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (E-tamo-hi-no-Mikoto) ''' Said to be the first Musashi-kuni-no-Miyatsuko (Governor of Musashi Province) from the Izumo clan, enshrined at the "Musashi Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Shrine" within the shrine grounds. '''Associated Deities (Haishi-shin)''' * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q382005}} (Sugawara no Michizane) – deity of learning and scholarship * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} (Amaterasu Ōmikami) – Sun goddess * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto) – deity of nation-building and medicine * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} (Izanagi-no-Mikoto) – Creator deity * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q682306}} (Izanami-no-Mikoto) – Creator deity * {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稻魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto) – deity of grain and food * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} (Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto) – Mountain deity * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵嶋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} (Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto) – One of the Three Munakata Goddesses * {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|速玉男命|Kumano Gongen=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}} (Hayatama-no-Kami) – God of life and exorcism * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事解之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} (Kotoshironushi-no-Kami) – God of resolution and harmony * {{ill|Oyabiko|ja|大屋毘古命|Oyabiko=Oyabiko|lt=Oyabiko-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyabiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Oyabiko-no-Kami) – Guardian deity of the land File:Izumoiwai-jinja(Iruma,Miyadera) BulletinBoard.jpg|Historical information board File:Izumoiwai-jinja(Iruma,Miyadera) Musashikokuzou-gosha.jpg|Musashi Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Shrine File:Izumoiwai-jinja(Iruma,Miyadera) Hengaku1.jpg|First torii plaque File:Izumoiwai-jinja(Iruma,Miyadera) Torii2 Hengaku.jpg|Second torii plaque File:Izumoiwai-jinja(Iruma,Miyadera) Honden Hengaku.jpg|Honden (Main Hall) plaque == Auxiliary Shrines (Sessha) == To the right when facing the main shrine: * '''Yakumo Shrine''' – Enshrining {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (God of storms and sea) To the left when facing the main shrine: * '''Gokoku Shrine''' – Enshrining {{ill|Takamimusubi|qid=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高御魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|1=Takamimusubi}} and war heroes File:Izumoiwai-jinja(Iruma,Miyadera) Yagumo-jinja.jpg|Yakumo Shrine File:Izumoiwai-jinja(Iruma,Miyadera) Gokoku-jinja.jpg|Gokoku Shrine == Cultural Properties == * '''1557 (Kōji 3) – Certificate of Protection (Shuinjō) by Hōjō Ujiyasu:''' Document issued by {{ill|Hōjō Ujiyasu|en|Hōjō Ujiyasu|qid=Q943643|lt=Hōjō Ujiyasu|1=Hōjō Ujiyasu}}, the lord of Odawara Castle, exempting the shrine from property taxes. :'''Hōjō Ujiyasu Certificate (Shuinjō):''' :''「出雲祝神社中、棟別之事、指置之畢横合之儀不可有候、依如件、弘治三年丁巳十一月廿七日、狩野大膳亮、庄式部少輔奉之」'' * '''1591 (Tenshō 19) – Shuinjō by Tokugawa Ieyasu:''' Issued by {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|qid=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} the year after his entry into the Kantō region, granting the shrine a stipend of 10 koku of land. == Annual Events == * '''Main Shrine Grand Festival''' (September 29) * '''Yakumo Shrine Grand Festival''' (July 15) * '''Gokoku Shrine Grand Festival''' (May 5) == Access == * From {{ill|Kotesashi Station|en|Kotesashi Station|qid=Q6433936|lt=Kotesashi Station|1=Kotesashi Station}} on the {{ill|Seibu Ikebukuro Line|en|Seibu Ikebukuro Line|qid=Q93546|lt=Seibu Ikebukuro Line|1=Seibu Ikebukuro Line}}, take the {{ill|Seibu Bus|ja|西武バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seibu Bus|1=Seibu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11628880}} bound for "Miyadera Nishi" and alight at "JA Miyadera Branch" stop. The shrine is about a 10-minute walk from the bus stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|出雲祝神社|version=91453748|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Izumoiwai Shrine (Iruma)}} [[ja:出雲祝神社]] {{wikidata link|Q70280025}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iruma, Saitama]] [[Category:Municipal History of Iruma]] [[Category:Musashi Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Pages with 50+ untranslated japanese characters]]
